title: Align existing documentation with implemented boundaries
outOfScope: Changing runtime behavior, guard policy semantics, release assets, or installation code.
Claiming provider-hosted identity, permissions, audit immutability, compliance certification, or universal semantic coverage.
Deleting historical plans or archived Work Item evidence.

riskAssessment.reason: Documentation-only alignment with no runtime or policy semantic changes; claims are checked against repository evidence and explicit exclusions.

executionDecision.reason: User-authorized documentation alignment follows the completed regression gate.

preReviewWarnings: Confirm terminology consistently identifies Repository Governance Layer boundaries and does not overclaim agent runtime or enterprise assurance.
Confirm English, Chinese, and Japanese documentation remain aligned without expanding the markdown complexity budget.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: README and architecture documents consistently describe AI Cockpit as a Repository Governance Layer, not an Agent Runtime, Sandbox, identity system, or enterprise compliance product.

Trust Layer, Guard, configuration, installation, upgrade, release, enterprise-boundary, fixture, and adopter lifecycle documentation agree on evidence ownership, fail-closed behavior, and supported boundaries.
English, Chinese, and Japanese public documentation do not make claims unsupported by repository-controlled evidence and preserve the finite known-scenario limitation.
Documentation metadata, markdown budget, link/terminology checks, and the full project quality suite pass without runtime or policy changes.
guidelines: Use repository-controlled evidence for public claims and state external-provider boundaries explicitly.
Keep terminology language-neutral and aligned with the canonical Trust Layer and glossary boundaries.
Do not expand claims beyond the finite deterministic fixtures and documented lifecycle evidence.

intent.problem: The implementation and governance boundaries have advanced through the preceding Work Items, while the existing public and multilingual documentation still contains inconsistent names, lifecycle wording, and evidence boundaries.
constraints: Documentation-only changes; do not alter runtime, policy semantics, release assets, or installation behavior.
Keep the Repository Governance Layer boundary explicit and avoid claims about model-internal state or enterprise assurance.
Keep additions concise so the enforced markdown complexity budget remains within its approved limit.
intent.rationale: A single terminology and boundary pass gives users one truthful explanation of the implemented controls before release publication.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: The user explicitly authorized continuous execution of the remediation plan, including documentation alignment and its full PR lifecycle.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Architecture and README use the canonical Repository Governance Layer boundary
Installation, upgrade, rollback, release, and adopter evidence ownership is consistent
Enterprise identity, permissions, audit, compliance, and model-internal claims remain explicitly bounded
Multilingual documentation uses aligned terminology without unsupported claims

rawUserRequest: 对齐现有文档，然后发布新版本并清理执行计划文档。

declaredIntent.summary: Align all existing public documentation with the implemented governance, evidence, lifecycle, and enterprise boundaries before release.
